title: Basic Calculator
description: Handles user input for two numbers and an operator, applying conditional logic (if/elif/else) to perform basic arithmetic operations.

title: Weight Converter
description: Takes user input for weight and unit (Lbs/Kgs), using conditional logic and basic mathematical operators to apply the correct conversion formula.

title: Temperature Converter
description: Converts temperatures between Celsius and Fahrenheit using standard formulas, focusing on float type casting and string formatting.

title: Compound Interest Calculator
description: Calculates compound interest over time. Utilizes while loops for input validation (preventing negative values) and exponentiation operators.

title: Countdown Timer
description: A time-based utility that utilizes the 'time' module to pause execution, demonstrating for-loops and string formatting for digital clock displays.

title: Shopping Cart Program
description: Simulates a checkout system by appending user inputs to parallel lists (items and prices) inside a while loop, then iterating to calculate a total.

title: Quiz Game
description: Iterates through a tuple of questions and a 2D tuple of options. Maps user answers against a tuple of correct answers to calculate a final score.

title: Banking Program
description: A state-driven application using custom functions for show_balance, deposit, and withdraw. Uses a while loop for the main menu navigation.

title: Slot Machine
description: Generates a randomized 3x3 grid using the 'random' module. Evaluates rows for matching symbols to calculate payouts based on a betting system.

title: Hangman Game
description: A classic word-guessing game. Uses dictionaries to store ASCII art states, the 'random' module to select words, and sets to track guessed letters.

title: Encryption Program
description: Implements a substitution cipher. Converts strings to lists, shuffles a character set using the 'random' module, and maps plain text to cipher text.

title: Weather App
description: Fetches live weather data using the 'requests' module. Parses JSON responses from a REST API and handles HTTP status codes and exceptions.
